kakao.shareDescription: What type are you when doing CrossFit? Take the test now!

og.defaultSubtitle: Discover your CrossFit MBTI with 16 questions

q1.question: What got you into CrossFit?
q1.optionA: I had a clear goal like fitness or health
q1.optionB: I just wanted to try something new

q2.question: During warm-up, I...
q2.optionA: Chat with the person next to me while stretching
q2.optionB: Roll out solo in the corner with a foam roller

q3.question: In a team WOD, when someone next to you is dying...
q3.optionA: "X reps left! Don't stop now!" — I count and encourage
q3.optionB: I'm gasping too hard to even count for them

q4.question: If you could know tomorrow's WOD in advance...
q4.optionA: Of course I'd check and plan my strategy
q4.optionB: Not knowing is more fun — I'll see when I get there

q5.question: You just hit a new PR! You...
q5.optionA: Scream so loud the box shakes and post it on Instagram
q5.optionB: Feel proud inside and quietly log it on my phone

q6.question: Today's WOD has 100 wall walks...
q6.optionA: Ugh... but I'll just get to the wall and start
q6.optionB: 100... let me pull out the calculator and figure out sets

q7.question: When buying CrossFit shoes, I...
q7.optionA: Compare grip, cushioning, and every review out there
q7.optionB: If the color's nice, I'm sold — just buy it

q8.question: Today's WOD has thrusters AND burpees...
q8.optionA: Thrusters or whatever — heading to the box now
q8.optionB: This is a sign from above — today is a rest day

q9.question: During rest after the WOD, I...
q9.optionA: Lie down chatting with the person next to me about how we almost died
q9.optionB: Sit in the corner, soul departed, staring at the rower

q10.question: When a new movement shows up in the WOD...
q10.optionA: Just try it and figure it out as I go

q11.question: When the coach says 'That form isn't right'...
q11.optionA: I ask the coach exactly what's wrong
q11.optionB: I get a bit flustered and just try to fix it

q12.question: A friend says 'Let's sign up for a CrossFit competition!'
q12.optionA: Set a date and immediately plan diet and training
q12.optionB: Haha sure, I'll think about it~ (and let it fade)

q13.question: When you're interested in someone at the box...
q13.optionA: Casually move closer and strike up a conversation
q13.optionB: Watch them train from afar, secretly

q14.question: A newcomer is struggling with a movement...
q14.optionA: Think 'that's not right...' but keep it to myself
q14.optionB: Go over and demonstrate while explaining the mechanics

q15.question: Someone says 'My numbers aren't improving lately...'
q15.optionA: How about changing your program? Maybe you need more volume
q15.optionB: You're doing great! Everyone hits a plateau sometimes

q16.question: A surprise box social dinner is announced!
q16.optionA: I mentally rehearse what to wear and what to talk about
q16.optionB: I'm shy around people... should I even go?

ISTJ.description: The human database of the box who logs every WOD down to the decimal in spreadsheets or apps. Hates deviating from routine and secretly stresses when equipment isn't put away properly.
traits: 5 years of WOD logs on file
ISTJ.quote: "If you didn't log it, it didn't happen."

ISFJ.description: The first to approach a nervous newcomer with a warm welcome. Cares more about everyone's safety and the box vibe than their own workout — the guardian angel of the box.
traits: Knows where the first aid kit is
Wipes chalk dust without being asked

INFJ.description: Sees exercise not as mere physical activity but as a journey of self-discovery. Ponders 'Why do I endure this suffering?' mid-WOD and occasionally drops wisdom deeper than the coach's.
traits: Workout journal reads like an essay
Meditates in the corner of the box
Asks the coach about the meaning behind movements

INTJ.description: Calculates optimal pace and rest intervals the moment the WOD is posted. Plans today's weight by working backward from a competition goal a year away — a true strategist.
traits: Has a separate personal training plan

ISTP.description: Quiet and seemingly indifferent, but eyes light up the moment they grip the barbell. Understands equipment mechanics and handles tools with skill — silently pushing limits while others chat.
traits: Keeps adding weight without a word

ISFP.description: Cares about box lighting, music, and workout outfit coordination as much as performance. Sometimes what matters more than the WOD score is how good the form looked and whether the post-workout selfie turned out.
traits: Knows every new workout apparel drop

INFP.description: Deeply moved by the bond with teammates during team WODs. Gets teary-eyed clapping when the last person finishes — a romantic who loves the tight-knit CrossFit community.
traits: One compliment from the coach = a week of happiness

INTP.description: Stays up all night studying the physics of Olympic lifts on YouTube. Mentally argues 'Well, anatomically speaking...' when the coach explains, but suffers because their body can't match their theory.
traits: Studying theory is more fun than working out
Analyzes specs of every new piece of equipment

ESTP.description: Body first, think later. If it looks doable, they try it immediately. Shakes off failure and gets right back up — the most energetic and action-oriented person in the box.

ESFP.description: Energy never drops from the moment they walk in until they leave. Dances and cracks jokes even during brutal WODs to keep the vibe up — sometimes enjoys the post-workout hangout more than the workout itself.
ESFP.quote: "If it's not fun, it's not CrossFit!"

ENFP.description: Shows up every day with new shoes and gear declaring 'TODAY is my day!' Burns bright and fades fast sometimes, but in that moment, nobody burns hotter — the spark plug of the box.
traits: Has been registered at 3 boxes simultaneously

ENTP.description: Constantly figuring out how to get more rounds with less effort. Finds loopholes in the rules to create efficient movements and loves debating movement standards with the coach.
ENTP.quote: "Even a No Rep is a strategy."

ESTJ.description: Absolutely will not tolerate fudged round counts or times. Strict with themselves and others — sometimes scarier than the coach when checking rounds. The order keeper of the box.
traits: Won't rest until the timer hits zero

ESFJ.description: Better at networking than working out. Handles box dinners, group competition sign-ups, birthday parties — making sure nobody feels left out. The social butterfly of the box.
ESFJ.quote: "Who's down for BBQ after the workout?"

ENFJ.description: Forgets about their own WOD because they're too busy cheering everyone else on. Has a gift for bringing out people's potential — when they're next to you, it feels like you can lift 20 lbs more.
traits: Voice is always hoarse from cheering
More excited about others' PRs than their own
ENFJ.worstWOD: Solo For Time (can't cheer for others)

ENTJ.description: Analyzes the box not just as a gym but as a business model. Points out operational inefficiencies and proposes improvements — secretly dreams of opening their own box someday.
traits: Always aims for 1st place in competitions
